title: Distress Tolerance Toolkit Checklist
description: Use this checklist during or after an emotional crisis to track which distress tolerance skills you've tried. Having multiple tools ready means you're never dependent on a single strategy. Review this list when you're calm so you know your options before a crisis hits.
instructions: When you're in emotional distress, work through this list and check off skills as you try them. You don't need to use all of them — find the ones that work for you. If distress is at 7+ out of 10, start with TIPP skills at the top.

items.label: TIPP: Temperature — held ice, splashed cold water on face, or used a cold pack
TIPP: Intense Exercise — did 1-3 minutes of fast movement (jumping, marching, running in place)
TIPP: Paced Breathing — breathed in for 4, out for 6-8, for at least 10 breaths
TIPP: Paired Muscle Relaxation — tensed and released muscle groups while breathing
Distraction: Did an engaging activity (cleaned, cooked, organized, drew, played music)
Distraction: Helped someone else or did something kind
Distraction: Used opposite emotions (funny video, uplifting music, comforting show)
Self-Soothing: Sight — looked at something beautiful, nature, photos, art
Self-Soothing: Sound — listened to calming music, nature sounds, or white noise
Self-Soothing: Smell — used a comforting scent (candle, lotion, essential oil, fresh air)
Self-Soothing: Taste — had a comforting drink or snack mindfully
Self-Soothing: Touch — used a soft blanket, warm bath, hand massage, or grounding texture
Wise Mind: Paused and asked 'What would Wise Mind say right now?'
Observe: Noticed my emotions without judging them or acting on them
Radical Acceptance: Acknowledged 'This is what is happening right now' without fighting it

disclaimer: This checklist supports crisis coping and is not a substitute for professional help. If distress tolerance skills are not enough and you are in danger of harming yourself or others, please reach out immediately: call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline), text HOME to 741741 (Crisis Text Line), or call 911.
